hypothesis: If a stereochemistry-aware diffusion model (LoQI) is trained on a large-scale dataset of neural-network-potential-optimized conformers (ChEMBL3D), then it will generate low-energy molecular conformers with near-quantum-mechanical accuracy, correct stereochemistry, and competitive RMSD to experimental crystal structures compared to existing methods, because encoding explicit stereochemical information into the graph representation and training on high-quality AIMNet2-optimized structures enables the model to learn the energy landscape of drug-like molecules at DFT-level fidelity.
